title: Llama7B attention/KV quality proxy for GQA and KV4
hypothesis: A controlled attention proxy can separate the risk of low-bit KV quantization from the larger risk of post-hoc K/V head sharing, so GQA8/KV4 should remain a quality experiment until native-GQA or retraining evidence exists.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Do GQA8/KV8 and GQA8/KV4 preserve attention top-1, retrieval hits, probability distribution, and value outputs under controlled long-context attention regimes?
include: correlated-head, retrieval, low-margin, and independent-head regimes
attention top-1 match, retrieval hit, KL divergence, output cosine, and output RMSE
exclude: claiming measured LLaMA perplexity or task accuracy
training native GQA or MQA weights
QAT or learned KV quantization scales

expected_benefit: checks whether KV4 alone is less risky than head sharing
tests whether GQA8/KV4 is plausible enough to justify a model-native quality run
keeps MQA/KV4 visible as a hardware bound while preventing accidental promotion
risks: post-hoc K/V head averaging is pessimistic for models trained natively with GQA
the proxy is synthetic and cannot replace trained-model perplexity or long-context retrieval tests
KV quantization uses simple symmetric scales rather than a learned or calibrated scheme

required_evaluations.objective: Run the controlled attention/KV quality proxy for GQA8 and KV4 candidates.

expected_result.reason: Use the proxy to decide whether the next expensive quality job should target model-native GQA8/KV8 or low-bit KV4 recovery.
